one_line_takeaway: UOT-Unlearn reframes class unlearning for one-step (flow-map) generative models as an Unbalanced Optimal Transport problem with a forget/retain cost trade-off, achieving markedly better PUL-vs-u-FID trade-offs than adapted diffusion-unlearning baselines on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-256 without needing real retain data.
key_finding: On CIFAR-10 (Table 2, Average rows across classes 1/6/8), UOT-Unlearn achieves the best PUL-vs-u-FID trade-off of all methods on both architectures: on CTM, Ours attains PUL 85.51% / u-FID 6.96 versus GA (PUL 74.21%, u-FID 147.00), SA (PUL 72.50%, u-FID 51.61), SalUn (PUL 60.82%, u-FID 42.02), and VDU (PUL 51.18%, u-FID 57.05), against an original (pretrained) FID of 4.64. On MeanFlow, Ours attains PUL 92.44% / u-FID 19.53 versus GA (79.92%, 70.69), SA (80.51%, 41.51), SalUn (75.83%, 32.72), and VDU (51.86%, 57.88), against an original FID of 8.01 — i.e., UOT-Unlearn has both the highest unlearning success and by far the lowest fidelity degradation among all compared methods on both backbones. On ImageNet-256 (class-conditional MeanFlow, target class 'goldfish', pretrained FID 11.57), UOT-Unlearn reaches PUL 85.08% with u-FID 20.16 (computed over 36 aquatic retain classes), while the GA baseline needs to inflate u-FID to 79.89 to achieve comparable suppression, illustrating a much worse efficacy-fidelity trade-off for the naive baseline. Ablations show an optimum at loss weight lambda=1.0 (both too-small lambda=0.1 and too-large lambda=5.0 hurt PUL and/or u-FID) and margin m=0.34 (m<=0.30 leaves residual forget-class semantics; m>=0.40 erodes neighboring retain classes).
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution (not related work) is UOT-Unlearn, the first class-unlearning framework designed specifically for one-step generative models (flow-map / consistency-style architectures such as CTM and MeanFlow, which map noise to data in a single forward pass). Prior diffusion unlearning methods (GA, Selective Amnesia, SalUn, VDU) intervene at intermediate denoising timesteps and therefore cannot be applied to one-step generators, which have no intermediate steps. The authors cast unlearning as an Unbalanced Optimal Transport (UOT) problem: the source distribution is the pretrained generator's output distribution and the target is the data distribution, with an unlearning cost c_ul that heavily penalizes samples falling within a 'forget region' (defined via cosine distance from a precomputed forget-class feature-space centroid mu_f) while imposing an L2 fidelity cost on samples outside that region. Using the semi-dual UOT/UOTM formulation, they derive a min-max training objective (dual potential v_phi vs. generator G_theta, Algorithm 1) that fine-tunes the pretrained one-step generator so that probability mass is smoothly redistributed away from the forget class into the remaining classes rather than collapsing into noise-like samples. Critically, the method is data-free at optimization time: it needs no real retain data, only generated samples from the pretrained model plus a one-time-computed forget centroid from a small set of real forget-class images (512 for CIFAR-10, 260 for ImageNet-256).
threat_model: This is not an adversarial-security paper with an attacker/defender threat model; it is a content-safety / machine-unlearning paper. The motivating risk stated by the authors is that as one-step generators become fast and widely deployable, they could rapidly produce undesirable content such as NSFW imagery or copyrighted material, and existing multi-step unlearning defenses cannot be applied to remove such concepts from one-step models. The 'threat' addressed is therefore unwanted/harmful content generation by a deployed generative model rather than an external adversary manipulating the model; there is no adversarial robustness, poisoning, or evasion analysis in the paper (nothing suggests the unlearning is evaluated against an adversary trying to recover the forgotten class, e.g., via prompt/latent search or relearning attacks).
limitations: The paper itself acknowledges/implies several limitations: (1) it is a preprint 'under review' with a single-class (not multi-concept or free-text-prompt) unlearning setting, evaluated only on class-conditional/unconditional image classifiers (CIFAR-10, ImageNet-256), not on text-to-image or NSFW-specific benchmarks despite motivating the work with NSFW/copyright risks. (2) The ImageNet-256 experiment restricts unlearning to a hand-chosen localized subset of 37 aquatic classes rather than the full 1,000-class conditioning space, which the authors say is 'intentionally designed' to aid redistribution — this narrows the demonstrated scalability claim. (3) Baselines (GA, SA, SalUn, VDU) were originally built for multi-step diffusion and had to be adapted/re-engineered by the authors for one-step models (e.g., SalUn's sparsity ratio was changed from the paper-recommended 50% to 95% because 50% collapsed one-step generation quality), so the baseline comparison reflects the authors' own re-implementations rather than published one-step baselines. (4) No adversarial evaluation of unlearning robustness (e.g., attempts to relearn or recover the forgotten class) is reported. (5) The KL/reverse-KL divergence choice and the nu ≈ p_pre approximation (used because real retain data is inaccessible) are motivated informally via mode-seeking intuition rather than proven bounds.

datasets_used: ImageNet-256 (37-class aquatic subset for conditional unlearning)
quantitative_results: CIFAR-10 CTM, Average over classes 1/6/8: original FID 4.64; UOT-Unlearn PUL 85.51%, u-FID 6.96
CIFAR-10 CTM, Average: Gradient Ascent PUL 74.21%, u-FID 147.00
CIFAR-10 CTM, Average: Selective Amnesia PUL 72.50%, u-FID 51.61
CIFAR-10 CTM, Average: SalUn PUL 60.82%, u-FID 42.02
CIFAR-10 CTM, Average: VDU PUL 51.18%, u-FID 57.05
CIFAR-10 MeanFlow, Average over classes 1/6/8: original FID 8.01; UOT-Unlearn PUL 92.44%, u-FID 19.53
CIFAR-10 MeanFlow, Average: Gradient Ascent PUL 79.92%, u-FID 70.69
CIFAR-10 MeanFlow, Average: Selective Amnesia PUL 80.51%, u-FID 41.51
CIFAR-10 MeanFlow, Average: SalUn PUL 75.83%, u-FID 32.72
CIFAR-10 MeanFlow, Average: VDU PUL 51.86%, u-FID 57.88
ImageNet-256 (goldfish, conditional MeanFlow/SiT-XL/2, pretrained FID 11.57): UOT-Unlearn PUL 85.08%, u-FID 20.16 (over 36 aquatic retain classes)
ImageNet-256: Gradient Ascent baseline inflates u-FID to 79.89 to achieve comparable concept suppression
Ablation (CTM, Class 8): best trade-off at forget-loss weight lambda=1.0 and margin m=0.34
Forget-anchor computation used 512 images (CIFAR-10) and 260 images = 20% of ~1,300 per-class images (ImageNet-256)
Classifier accuracies used for PUL: DenseNet-121 94.06% (CIFAR-10), ViT-L/16 88.55% (ImageNet-256)
